posts.title: Fail Safe Logic
posts.author: Admin
posts.excerpt: Fail Safe Logic in each project often not goes to advance level.
posts.content:
Introduction

In almost all the project, fail safe design and logic is given less importance and carried out only for customer satisfaction. Any Fail Safe logic must have details like which Safety Category has been adopted, how it is designed and how it is going to be executed. Customer will be more curious and will realize how serious are we about safety. Wiring E-Stop to safety relay or PLC often goes with theory logic. This must be analyzed in detail, designer need to adopt a system to understand what type of safety ,customer requires or if he is not aware, based on project 3D CAD needs to present safety category adopted in design.

posts.title: Material BOM Tracker — Sample Sheet
posts.author: Admin
posts.excerpt: Download this sample sheet to plan BOM, PR, PO and material receipt dates before entering them into the Material Procurement Tracker.
posts.content:
Material BOM Tracker — Sample Sheet

Use this template to plan procurement before entering data into the tracker app

Getting PLC, HMI, Servo, and other critical materials on time is essential to completing a project on schedule. This sheet helps you plan out BOM, PR, PO, and Material Receipt dates for every item before logging them into the Material Procurement Tracker.

What's in the sheet

  • 12 sample materials (PLC, HMI, Servo Drive/Motor, Safety Relay, E-Stop, etc.)
  • Columns for BOM Created, PR Created, PR Approved, PO Created, PO Approved, Target Receipt, Actual Receipt
  • A second "Instructions" tab explaining the date format and alert rules

Automatic Alert Rules in the Tracker

  • Red flag — PR Created not filled within 3 days of BOM Created date
  • Red flag — PO Created not filled within 3 days of PR Approved date
  • Red flag — Target Receipt date passed without Actual Receipt logged
  • Amber — Material due within 3 days of Target Receipt

How to use it

  1. Download the sheet below
  2. Fill in your project's material list and dates (YYYY-MM-DD format)
  3. Open the Material Procurement Tracker page
  4. Select or add your project, paste the BOM file's link into "BOM Location"
  5. Manually add each material into the tracker with its dates — the tracker will flag any item that's overdue automatically
📥 Download Sample BOM Tracker Sheet

Opens Excel file directly from GitHub

Industrial Automation Controls Team • Material Procurement Tracking

posts.title: Push Button and Indicators Detail Form
posts.author: Admin
posts.excerpt: Download this form ,fill details and send it to Electrical and Mechanical Designer. In case of any input needed from Customer, share the form with your manager and get the details.
posts.content:
Push Buttons, Indicators & Tower Lamps

Requirement Form — Special Purpose Machines (SPM)

Project Information

Field Details
Project Name
Project Number
Customer Name
Site Location
Date

1. Push Buttons

Type Color Function Qty Make/Model
Start ■ Green Machine Start / Cycle Start
Stop ■ Red Machine Stop
E-Stop ■ Red (Mushroom) Emergency Stop
Reset ■ Blue Fault Reset

2. Indicator Lamps

Indicator Color Meaning Qty
Power ON ■ White Supply healthy
Running ■ Green Machine in cycle
Fault ■ Red Alarm / Trip
Warning ■ Amber Caution / Standby

3. Tower Lamps

Layer Color Condition Mode (Steady/Flash)
Top ■ Red Fault / Emergency Flash
Middle ■ Amber Warning / Idle Steady
Bottom ■ Green Running OK Steady

Best Practices — IEC 60204-1 Standard Colors

  • Green — Start / Running / Process OK
  • Red — Stop / Emergency / Fault
  • Yellow / Amber — Warning / Standby
  • Blue — Reset / Information
  • White — Power ON / Idle
